summary: The diff implements most of the requested requirements across REQ-1 through REQ-5. YouTube spinner fix (REQ-1), bug report button (REQ-2), in-app download (REQ-3), user ID messaging + reply (REQ-4), and pause/resume (REQ-5) are all substantially addressed. However, several specific acceptance criteria have gaps: the REQ-3 message tab mechanism appears to be a detail panel rather than a true tab, the useDownloadClip anchor download mechanism is not confirmed, REQ-5's admin login prompt for unauthenticated toggle attempts is not clearly implemented, and the backend method names differ from the specified setPauseState/getAppStatus.

missing_requirements.requirement: REQ-3: The download button in ClipCard uses the useDownloadClip hook result to trigger the download without navigation via a programmatic anchor element with the download attribute
missing_requirements.reason: ClipCard.tsx imports and calls useDownloadClip, but the diff is truncated before showing the actual download trigger mechanism. It is not confirmed that the hook uses a temporary anchor element with the download attribute rather than redirecting externally.

missing_requirements.requirement: REQ-5: If an unauthenticated or non-admin user tries to access the pause/resume toggle, they must be shown an admin login prompt
missing_requirements.reason: SystemControls.tsx shows a toggle for admins, but there is no evidence of an admin login prompt being shown to unauthenticated or non-admin users who attempt to access the toggle. AdminPanel.tsx shows an Access Denied message but not a login prompt.

missing_requirements.requirement: REQ-5: The backend exposes setPauseState(paused: Bool) and getAppStatus() methods by those exact names, protected by admin authorization
missing_requirements.reason: The backend diff shows togglePauseSystem() and getSystemStatus() methods rather than the specified setPauseState(paused: Bool) and getAppStatus() method signatures.

missing_requirements.requirement: The backend AdminMessage type or a new Message type supports a reply/thread structure with replies stored and visible in the message thread
missing_requirements.reason: The migration.mo and backend/main.mo show AdminMessage with fromUserId/toUserId fields added and there is a replyToMessage call in UserMessages.tsx, but no evidence of a thread or replies array field in AdminMessage or a dedicated reply type in the backend to store threaded replies.

hallucinated_features.description: A dedicated MessagesPage.tsx page component was added as a separate page route at /messages
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asked to update the UserMessages component behavior such as opening messages in tabs and adding a reply feature. A full new page route was not explicitly requested.

hallucinated_features.description: A useMyMessages.ts hook was added as a separate reusable hook
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request did not explicitly request a new useMyMessages hook. This appears to be additional infrastructure not specified in the requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: A useSendAdminMessage.ts hook was added as a separate reusable hook
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request did not explicitly request a new useSendAdminMessage hook separate from the existing admin messaging flow.
